speaker: HAMLET
text: So much for this, sir. Now shall you see the other.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You do remember all the circumstance?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Remember it, my lord!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sir, in my heart there was a kind of fighting

speaker: HAMLET
text: That would not let me sleep. Methought I lay

speaker: HAMLET
text: Worse than the mutines in the bilboes. Rashly—

speaker: HAMLET
text: And praised be rashness for it; let us know,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Our indiscretion sometime serves us well

speaker: HAMLET
text: When our deep plots do pall; and that should learn us

speaker: HAMLET
text: There’s a divinity that shapes our ends,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Rough-hew them how we will—

speaker: HORATIO
text: That is most certain.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Up from my cabin,

speaker: HAMLET
text: My sea-gown scarfed about me, in the dark

speaker: HAMLET
text: Groped I to find out them; had my desire,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Fingered their packet, and in fine withdrew

speaker: HAMLET
text: To mine own room again, making so bold

speaker: HAMLET
text: (My fears forgetting manners) to unfold

speaker: HAMLET
text: Their grand commission; where I found, Horatio,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A royal knavery—an exact command,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Larded with many several sorts of reasons

speaker: HAMLET
text: Importing Denmark’s health and England’s too,

speaker: HAMLET
text: With—ho!—such bugs and goblins in my life,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That on the supervise, no leisure bated,

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, not to stay the grinding of the ax,

speaker: HAMLET
text: My head should be struck off.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Is ’t possible?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Here’s the commission. Read it at more leisure.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But wilt thou hear now how I did proceed?

speaker: HORATIO
text: I beseech you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Being thus benetted round with villainies,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or I could make a prologue to my brains,

speaker: HAMLET
text: They had begun the play. I sat me down,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Devised a new commission, wrote it fair—

speaker: HAMLET
text: I once did hold it, as our statists do,

speaker: HAMLET
text: A baseness to write fair, and labored much

speaker: HAMLET
text: How to forget that learning; but, sir, now

speaker: HAMLET
text: It did me yeoman’s service. Wilt thou know

speaker: HAMLET
text: Th’ effect of what I wrote?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Ay, good my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: An earnest conjuration from the King,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As England was his faithful tributary,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As love between them like the palm might flourish,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As peace should still her wheaten garland wear

speaker: HAMLET
text: And stand a comma ’tween their amities,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And many suchlike ases of great charge,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That, on the view and knowing of these contents,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Without debatement further, more or less,

speaker: HAMLET
text: He should those bearers put to sudden death,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Not shriving time allowed.

speaker: HORATIO
text: How was this sealed?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, even in that was heaven ordinant.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I had my father’s signet in my purse,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Which was the model of that Danish seal;

speaker: HAMLET
text: Folded the writ up in the form of th’ other,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Subscribed it, gave ’t th’ impression, placed it safely,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The changeling never known. Now, the next day

speaker: HAMLET
text: Was our sea-fight; and what to this was sequent

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thou knowest already.

speaker: HORATIO
text: So Guildenstern and Rosencrantz go to ’t.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, man, they did make love to this employment.

speaker: HAMLET
text: They are not near my conscience. Their defeat

speaker: HAMLET
text: Does by their own insinuation grow.

speaker: HAMLET
text: ’Tis dangerous when the baser nature comes

speaker: HAMLET
text: Between the pass and fell incensèd points

speaker: HAMLET
text: Of mighty opposites.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Why, what a king is this!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Does it not, think thee, stand me now upon—

speaker: HAMLET
text: He that hath killed my king and whored my mother,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Popped in between th’ election and my hopes,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thrown out his angle for my proper life,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And with such cozenage—is ’t not perfect conscience

speaker: HAMLET
text: To quit him with this arm? And is ’t not to be damned

speaker: HAMLET
text: To let this canker of our nature come

speaker: HAMLET
text: In further evil?

speaker: HORATIO
text: It must be shortly known to him from England

speaker: HORATIO
text: What is the issue of the business there.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It will be short. The interim’s mine,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And a man’s life’s no more than to say “one.”

speaker: HAMLET
text: But I am very sorry, good Horatio,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That to Laertes I forgot myself,

speaker: HAMLET
text: For by the image of my cause I see

speaker: HAMLET
text: The portraiture of his. I’ll court his favors.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But, sure, the bravery of his grief did put me

speaker: HAMLET
text: Into a tow’ring passion.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Peace, who comes here?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Your Lordship is right welcome back to

speaker: OSRIC
text: Denmark.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I humbly thank you, sir.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Dost know this waterfly?

speaker: HORATIO
text: No, my good lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thy state is the more gracious,

speaker: HAMLET
text: for ’tis a vice to know him. He hath much

speaker: HAMLET
text: land, and fertile. Let a beast be lord of beasts and his

speaker: HAMLET
text: crib shall stand at the king’s mess. ’Tis a chough,

speaker: HAMLET
text: but, as I say, spacious in the possession of dirt.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Sweet lord, if your Lordship were at leisure, I

speaker: OSRIC
text: should impart a thing to you from his Majesty.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will receive it, sir, with all diligence of

speaker: HAMLET
text: spirit. Put your bonnet to his right use: ’tis for the

speaker: HAMLET
text: head.

speaker: OSRIC
text: I thank your Lordship; it is very hot.

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, believe me, ’tis very cold; the wind is

speaker: HAMLET
text: northerly.

speaker: OSRIC
text: It is indifferent cold, my lord, indeed.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But yet methinks it is very sultry and hot for

speaker: HAMLET
text: my complexion.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Exceedingly, my lord; it is very sultry, as

speaker: OSRIC
text: ’twere—I cannot tell how. My lord, his Majesty

speaker: OSRIC
text: bade me signify to you that he has laid a great wager

speaker: OSRIC
text: on your head. Sir, this is the matter—

speaker: HAMLET
text: I beseech you, remember.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Nay, good my lord, for my ease, in good faith.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Sir, here is newly come to court Laertes—believe

speaker: OSRIC
text: me, an absolute gentleman, full of most excellent

speaker: OSRIC
text: differences, of very soft society and great showing.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Indeed, to speak feelingly of him, he is the card or

speaker: OSRIC
text: calendar of gentry, for you shall find in him the

speaker: OSRIC
text: continent of what part a gentleman would see.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sir, his definement suffers no perdition in

speaker: HAMLET
text: you, though I know to divide him inventorially

speaker: HAMLET
text: would dozy th’ arithmetic of memory, and yet but

speaker: HAMLET
text: yaw neither, in respect of his quick sail. But, in the

speaker: HAMLET
text: verity of extolment, I take him to be a soul of great

speaker: HAMLET
text: article, and his infusion of such dearth and rareness

speaker: HAMLET
text: as, to make true diction of him, his semblable is his

speaker: HAMLET
text: mirror, and who else would trace him, his umbrage,

speaker: HAMLET
text: nothing more.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Your Lordship speaks most infallibly of him.

speaker: HAMLET
text: The concernancy, sir? Why do we wrap the

speaker: HAMLET
text: gentleman in our more rawer breath?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Sir?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Is ’t not possible to understand in another

speaker: HORATIO
text: tongue? You will to ’t, sir, really.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What imports the nomination of

speaker: HAMLET
text: this gentleman?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Of Laertes?

speaker: HORATIO
text: His purse is empty already; all ’s golden words

speaker: HORATIO
text: are spent.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Of him, sir.

speaker: OSRIC
text: I know you are not ignorant—

speaker: HAMLET
text: I would you did, sir. Yet, in faith, if you did, it

speaker: HAMLET
text: would not much approve me. Well, sir?

speaker: OSRIC
text: You are not ignorant of what excellence Laertes

speaker: OSRIC
text: is—

speaker: HAMLET
text: I dare not confess that, lest I should compare

speaker: HAMLET
text: with him in excellence. But to know a man well

speaker: HAMLET
text: were to know himself.

speaker: OSRIC
text: I mean, sir, for his weapon. But in the imputation

speaker: OSRIC
text: laid on him by them, in his meed he’s

speaker: OSRIC
text: unfellowed.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What’s his weapon?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Rapier and dagger.

speaker: HAMLET
text: That’s two of his weapons. But, well—

speaker: OSRIC
text: The King, sir, hath wagered with him six Barbary

speaker: OSRIC
text: horses, against the which he has impawned, as I

speaker: OSRIC
text: take it, six French rapiers and poniards, with their

speaker: OSRIC
text: assigns, as girdle, hangers, and so. Three of the

speaker: OSRIC
text: carriages, in faith, are very dear to fancy, very

speaker: OSRIC
text: responsive to the hilts, most delicate carriages, and

speaker: OSRIC
text: of very liberal conceit.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What call you the “carriages”?

speaker: HORATIO
text: I knew you must be edified by the margent

speaker: HORATIO
text: ere you had done.

speaker: OSRIC
text: The carriages, sir, are the hangers.

speaker: HAMLET
text: The phrase would be more germane to the

speaker: HAMLET
text: matter if we could carry a cannon by our sides. I

speaker: HAMLET
text: would it might be “hangers” till then. But on. Six

speaker: HAMLET
text: Barbary horses against six French swords, their

speaker: HAMLET
text: assigns, and three liberal-conceited carriages—

speaker: HAMLET
text: that’s the French bet against the Danish. Why is this

speaker: HAMLET
text: all “impawned,” as you call it?

speaker: OSRIC
text: The King, sir, hath laid, sir, that in a dozen

speaker: OSRIC
text: passes between yourself and him, he shall not

speaker: OSRIC
text: exceed you three hits. He hath laid on twelve for

speaker: OSRIC
text: nine, and it would come to immediate trial if your

speaker: OSRIC
text: Lordship would vouchsafe the answer.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How if I answer no?

speaker: OSRIC
text: I mean, my lord, the opposition of your person

speaker: OSRIC
text: in trial.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sir, I will walk here in the hall. If it please his

speaker: HAMLET
text: Majesty, it is the breathing time of day with me. Let

speaker: HAMLET
text: the foils be brought, the gentleman willing, and the

speaker: HAMLET
text: King hold his purpose, I will win for him, an I can.

speaker: HAMLET
text: If not, I will gain nothing but my shame and the odd

speaker: HAMLET
text: hits.

speaker: OSRIC
text: Shall I deliver you e’en so?

speaker: HAMLET
text: To this effect, sir, after what flourish your

speaker: HAMLET
text: nature will.

speaker: OSRIC
text: I commend my duty to your Lordship.

speaker: HAMLET
text:
Yours.
                 
                Osric
                 
                exits
                .
               He does well to commend

speaker: HAMLET
text: it himself. There are no tongues else for ’s

speaker: HAMLET
text: turn.

text: This lapwing runs away with the shell on his

speaker: HORATIO
text: head.

speaker: HAMLET
text: He did comply, sir, with his dug before he

speaker: HAMLET
text: sucked it. Thus has he (and many more of the same

speaker: HAMLET
text: breed that I know the drossy age dotes on) only got

speaker: HAMLET
text: the tune of the time, and, out of an habit of

speaker: HAMLET
text: encounter, a kind of yeasty collection, which carries

speaker: HAMLET
text: them through and through the most fanned

speaker: HAMLET
text: and winnowed opinions; and do but blow them to

text: My lord, his Majesty commended him to you by

text: young Osric, who brings back to him that you

text: attend him in the hall. He sends to know if your

speaker: LORD
text: pleasure hold to play with Laertes, or that you will

speaker: LORD
text: take longer time.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I am constant to my purposes. They follow

speaker: HAMLET
text: the King’s pleasure. If his fitness speaks, mine is

speaker: HAMLET
text: ready now or whensoever, provided I be so able as

speaker: HAMLET
text: now.

speaker: LORD
text: The King and Queen and all are coming down.

speaker: HAMLET
text: In happy time.

speaker: LORD
text: The Queen desires you to use some gentle

speaker: LORD
text: entertainment to Laertes before you fall to play.

speaker: HAMLET
text: She well instructs me.

speaker: HORATIO
text: You will lose, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I do not think so. Since he went into France, I

speaker: HAMLET
text: have been in continual practice. I shall win at the

speaker: HAMLET
text: odds; but thou wouldst not think how ill all’s here

speaker: HAMLET
text: about my heart. But it is no matter.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Nay, good my lord—

speaker: HAMLET
text: It is but foolery, but it is such a kind of

speaker: HAMLET
text: gaingiving as would perhaps trouble a woman.

speaker: HORATIO
text: If your mind dislike anything, obey it. I will

speaker: HORATIO
text: forestall their repair hither and say you are not fit.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Not a whit. We defy augury. There is a

speaker: HAMLET
text: special providence in the fall of a sparrow. If it be

speaker: HAMLET
text: now, ’tis not to come; if it be not to come, it will be

speaker: HAMLET
text: now; if it be not now, yet it will come. The

speaker: HAMLET
text: readiness is all. Since no man of aught he leaves

speaker: HAMLET
text: knows, what is ’t to leave betimes? Let be.

speaker: KING
text: Come, Hamlet, come and take this hand from me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Give me your pardon, sir. I have done you wrong;

speaker: HAMLET
text: But pardon ’t as you are a gentleman. This presence knows,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And you must needs have heard, how I am punished

speaker: HAMLET
text: With a sore distraction. What I have done

speaker: HAMLET
text: That might your nature, honor, and exception

speaker: HAMLET
text: Roughly awake, I here proclaim was madness.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Was ’t Hamlet wronged Laertes? Never Hamlet.

speaker: HAMLET
text: If Hamlet from himself be ta’en away,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And when he’s not himself does wrong Laertes,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then Hamlet does it not; Hamlet denies it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Who does it, then? His madness. If ’t be so,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hamlet is of the faction that is wronged;

speaker: HAMLET
text: His madness is poor Hamlet’s enemy.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sir, in this audience

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let my disclaiming from a purposed evil

speaker: HAMLET
text: Free me so far in your most generous thoughts

speaker: HAMLET
text: That I have shot my arrow o’er the house

speaker: HAMLET
text: And hurt my brother.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I am satisfied in nature,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Whose motive in this case should stir me most

speaker: LAERTES
text: To my revenge; but in my terms of honor

speaker: LAERTES
text: I stand aloof and will no reconcilement

speaker: LAERTES
text: Till by some elder masters of known honor

speaker: LAERTES
text: I have a voice and precedent of peace

speaker: LAERTES
text: To keep my name ungored. But till that time

speaker: LAERTES
text: I do receive your offered love like love

speaker: LAERTES
text: And will not wrong it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I embrace it freely

speaker: HAMLET
text: And will this brothers’ wager frankly play.—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Give us the foils. Come on.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Come, one for me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll be your foil, Laertes; in mine ignorance

text: Your skill shall, like a star i’ th’ darkest night,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Stick fiery off indeed.

speaker: LAERTES

speaker: HAMLET

speaker: KING
text: Give them the foils, young Osric. Cousin Hamlet,

text: You know the wager?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Very well, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Your Grace has laid the odds o’ th’ weaker side.

speaker: KING
text: I do not fear it; I have seen you both.

speaker: KING
text: But, since he is better, we have therefore odds.

speaker: LAERTES
text: This is too heavy. Let me see another.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This likes me well. These foils have all a length?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Ay, my good lord.

text: Set me the stoups of wine upon that table.—

speaker: KING

text: Or quit in answer of the third exchange,

speaker: KING

text: And in the cup an union shall he throw,

speaker: KING
text: Richer than that which four successive kings

text: In Denmark’s crown have worn. Give me the cups,

text: And let the kettle to the trumpet speak,

text: The trumpet to the cannoneer without,

speaker: KING

speaker: LAERTES

speaker: LAERTES

Come.

speaker: LAERTES
text: A touch, a touch. I do confess ’t.

speaker: KING
text: Our son shall win.

speaker: QUEEN
text: He’s fat and scant of breath.—

text: Here, Hamlet, take my napkin; rub thy brows.

speaker: QUEEN
text: The Queen carouses to thy fortune, Hamlet.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Good madam.

text: Gertrude, do not drink.

text: I dare not drink yet, madam—by and by.

text: Come, let me wipe thy face.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come, for the third, Laertes. You do but dally.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I am afeard you make a wanton of me.

text: Nothing neither way.

speaker: LAERTES

speaker: HAMLET
text: How does the Queen?

text: She swoons to see them bleed.

speaker: QUEEN
text: No, no, the drink, the drink! O, my dear Hamlet!

speaker: QUEEN
text: The drink, the drink! I am poisoned.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Treachery! Seek it out.

speaker: LAERTES
text: It is here, Hamlet. Hamlet, thou art slain.

speaker: LAERTES
text: No med’cine in the world can do thee good.

speaker: LAERTES
text: In thee there is not half an hour’s life.

speaker: LAERTES
text: The treacherous instrument is in thy hand,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Unbated and envenomed. The foul practice

text: Hath turned itself on me. Lo, here I lie,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Never to rise again. Thy mother’s poisoned.

speaker: LAERTES

speaker: HAMLET
text: The point envenomed too! Then, venom, to thy work.

speaker: ALL
text: Treason, treason!

speaker: KING
text: O, yet defend me, friends! I am but hurt.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Here, thou incestuous, murd’rous, damnèd Dane,

text: Drink off this potion. Is thy union here?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Follow my mother.

speaker: LAERTES
text: He is justly served.

speaker: LAERTES
text: It is a poison tempered by himself.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Exchange forgiveness with me, noble Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Mine and my father’s death come not upon thee,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Nor thine on me.

text: Heaven make thee free of it. I follow thee.—

speaker: HAMLET
text: I am dead, Horatio.—Wretched queen, adieu.—

text: You that look pale and tremble at this chance,

text: That are but mutes or audience to this act,

text: Thou livest; report me and my cause aright

text: Here’s yet some liquor left.

speaker: HAMLET
text: As thou ’rt a man,

text: O God, Horatio, what a wounded name,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Things standing thus unknown, shall I leave behind me!

speaker: HAMLET
text: If thou didst ever hold me in thy heart,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Absent thee from felicity awhile

speaker: HAMLET
text: And in this harsh world draw thy breath in pain

speaker: HAMLET
text: To tell my story.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What warlike noise is this?

speaker: OSRIC
text: Young Fortinbras, with conquest come from Poland,

speaker: OSRIC
text: To th’ ambassadors of England gives

speaker: OSRIC
text: This warlike volley.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, I die, Horatio!

speaker: HAMLET
text: The potent poison quite o’ercrows my spirit.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I cannot live to hear the news from England.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But I do prophesy th’ election lights

speaker: HAMLET
text: On Fortinbras; he has my dying voice.

speaker: HAMLET
text: So tell him, with th’ occurrents, more and less,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Which have solicited—the rest is silence.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, O, O, O!

speaker: HORATIO
text: Now cracks a noble heart. Good night, sweet prince,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And flights of angels sing thee to thy rest.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Why does the drum come hither?

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Where is this sight?

speaker: HORATIO
text: What is it you would see?

speaker: HORATIO
text: If aught of woe or wonder, cease your search.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: This quarry cries on havoc. O proud Death,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: What feast is toward in thine eternal cell

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: That thou so many princes at a shot

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: So bloodily hast struck?

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: The sight is dismal,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: And our affairs from England come too late.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: The ears are senseless that should give us hearing

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: To tell him his commandment is fulfilled,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: That Rosencrantz and Guildenstern are dead.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Where should we have our thanks?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Not from his mouth,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Had it th’ ability of life to thank you.

speaker: HORATIO
text: He never gave commandment for their death.

speaker: HORATIO
text: But since, so jump upon this bloody question,

speaker: HORATIO
text: You from the Polack wars, and you from England,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Are here arrived, give order that these bodies

speaker: HORATIO
text: High on a stage be placed to the view,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And let me speak to th’ yet unknowing world

speaker: HORATIO
text: How these things came about. So shall you hear

speaker: HORATIO
text: Of carnal, bloody, and unnatural acts,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Of accidental judgments, casual slaughters,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Of deaths put on by cunning and forced cause,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And, in this upshot, purposes mistook

speaker: HORATIO
text: Fall’n on th’ inventors’ heads. All this can I

speaker: HORATIO
text: Truly deliver.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Let us haste to hear it

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: And call the noblest to the audience.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: For me, with sorrow I embrace my fortune.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: I have some rights of memory in this kingdom,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Which now to claim my vantage doth invite me.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Of that I shall have also cause to speak,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And from his mouth whose voice will draw on more.

speaker: HORATIO
text: But let this same be presently performed

speaker: HORATIO
text: Even while men’s minds are wild, lest more mischance

speaker: HORATIO
text: On plots and errors happen.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Let four captains

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Bear Hamlet like a soldier to the stage,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: For he was likely, had he been put on,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: To have proved most royal; and for his passage,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: The soldier’s music and the rite of war

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Speak loudly for him.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Take up the bodies. Such a sight as this

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Becomes the field but here shows much amiss.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Go, bid the soldiers shoot.
